history.summary: Overview tab gets a status grid + dedicated Error Reporting frame. Operator wanted: status of APIs and services, security stance, MCP server availability, sub-agent availability -- and a dedicated error reporting frame with JSON export of the errors associated with the displayed entries. v0.8.7 replaces the AUTHORED SURFACES legend footer with a 2x2 grid of cards: APIs and Services (service / admin API / browser surface / beacon / bind), Security Stance (security protocols / AI autonomy / open LAN access / governance posture / live findings), MCP Servers (count + reachable + first-4 preview), Sub-Agents (count + reachable + active leases + first-4 preview). Each card has a status dot in the title row colored good (green) / warn (amber) / crit (red) / neutral (gray) by the same rules used on the Telemetry-tab dots. Below the grid sits the Error Reporting frame: a scrolling list of recent error-flagged events with a per-row severity dot, kind/timestamp, message, and source. An Export to JSON button serializes the visible set to %PUBLIC%\Documents\Master Control Orchestration Server\error-export-<timestamp>.json -- mcos.error-export.v1 schema: { exportedAtUtc, count, events:[{id,timestampUtc,kind,severity,source,statusCode,message,detail}] }. Errors are harvested by ShellRuntime::CaptureSnapshot from /api/activity, filtered to events with statusCode>=400 OR kind/message containing error/failure/Unable. Capped at 50 most recent.

feat(shell): ShellRuntime::CaptureSnapshot now harvests recent errors at the tail of the snapshot pass. Calls FetchActivityEvents(L""), filters to statusCode>=400 OR kind/message containing 'error'/'failure'/'failed'/'Unable' or starting with 'ERROR'/'FATAL'. Caps the result at 50 entries.

history.summary: Sub-agent reachability probe is no longer IPv4-only. Pre-v0.8.2 AdminApiService::snapshot's per-sub-agent reachability probe used a bare AF_INET socket + inet_pton(AF_INET, ...) -- the seven default inventory entries (sentinel, architect, forge, scribe, recon, nexus, watchtower) advertise IPv6 ULA hosts (fde3:c02c:3afa:4572:6687:be77:e9f1:32c8) so inet_pton silently failed parse and every dot painted red on a stock install. v0.8.2 rewrites the probe around getaddrinfo with AF_UNSPEC hints: every address family the resolver returns is tried until one connects or all exhaust the 200 ms-per-address timeout. IPv4, IPv6, and DNS-name hosts all probe correctly. endpointHostPort now bracket-wraps IPv6 literals per RFC 3986 (closing the v0.7.6 known issue) so the dashboard reads e.g. '[fde3:...:32c8]:7201' instead of the visually-ambiguous 'fde3:...:32c8:7201'.
entries: fix(runtime): AdminApiService::snapshot reachability probe rewritten on getaddrinfo. AF_UNSPEC hints accept both AF_INET and AF_INET6; the loop tries every resolved address with non-blocking connect + 200 ms select, succeeding on the first reachable family. Both fd_set writeSet and errorSet are passed to select so a connection refusal lands in error rather than write -- avoids spurious reachable=true on hosts that explicitly refused.
fix(runtime): endpointHostPort string now wraps IPv6 literals in brackets ('[fde3:...:32c8]:7201') per RFC 3986. v0.7.6 known issue (recorded in VERSION.json) is closed.

history.summary: Three operator-driven changes landed in one release. (1) Application palette switched from Sam-Flynn cyan to Tron CLU red-orange. ShellAccentBrush moves from #00FFFF to #FF3D2E and every cyan-derived hex literal across App.xaml, MainWindow.xaml, every section's XAML, and the imperative ColorHelper.FromArgb calls in MainWindow.xaml.cpp / RuntimeSectionControl.xaml.cpp shifts to the matching red-orange tone. Status semantic colors (success green-mint, warning amber, danger pink-red) are intentionally preserved so a critical-state dot remains visually distinct from chrome on a red-themed UI. (2) Background curves replaced with a proper Tron grid. Pre-v0.8.1 the shell painted two large soft-stroke Ellipses behind the foreground content -- the operator called this out as 'not Tron styling due to the curves.' v0.8.1 removes both Ellipses and populates the same Canvas imperatively with a perpendicular line grid (vertical lines every 80 px, horizontal every 60 px, every fifth line emphasized in the soft accent brush) that resizes with the window via SizeChanged. (3) Cross-section drag on the Telemetry tab now works. v0.8.0 rejected drops where source and target lived in different section grids; v0.8.1 lifts that restriction by physically reparenting both tiles between section grids and swapping their attached Grid.Row / Grid.Column so the visual swap looks the same as a same-section drag. Persistence now records the sectionId per tile so a cross-section move survives shell restart -- on Loaded LoadAndApplyLayout reparents any tile whose persisted sectionId differs from its catalog default. Also creates docs/agent-decision-log.md as a session-durable, git-versioned memory anchor for the agent driving these releases.

ShellBackgroundBrush gradient and ShellHeroBrush retinted from cyan-blacks to red-blacks (#1A0405 / #3A0808 / #220A0A / #2A0D0D). Text brushes pivoted from cool (#9ECCDA / #7BA4B0 / #F0FFFF) to warm (#E8B89E / #B68870 / #FFEEDC) so foreground text sits properly on a red-toned background.

fix(shell): Telemetry tab cross-section drag is no longer rejected. Tile_Drop now physically reparents source and target between section grids when their parent Grids differ -- source moves into target's section grid taking target's row/col, target moves into source's old grid taking source's old row/col. Same-section drops still execute the in-place attached-property swap. tileLayout_ entries swap their sectionId fields so PersistLayout records the cross-section move.

fix(shell): TelemetrySectionControl::LoadAndApplyLayout now physically reparents tiles on startup when the persisted sectionId differs from the grid that currently owns the tile (i.e. cross-section moves survive shell restart). ResolveSectionGridForTile and ResolveSectionIdForTile prefer the live tileLayout_ state's sectionId over the static catalog metadata so all subsequent operations (drag, detach, reattach, hide) route to the new section.

history.summary: Telemetry tab is now operator-customizable. Pre-v0.8.0 the Telemetry view was a fixed three-section grid: Live Host Pressure (6 tiles), Resource Allocation (4 tiles), Operational Activity (5 tiles), with positions baked into XAML. v0.8.0 makes every tile (1) togglable via a Customize Layout flyout that lists every tile with a checkbox, (2) drag-reorderable inside its section by clicking and dragging the tile to another tile's position (positions swap), and (3) detachable to its own desktop window via a small detach button on each tile's title row. Detached tiles continue updating at the same 1Hz cadence as the main grid because the live-snapshot tick now broadcasts to the cached Telemetry section regardless of which tab the operator is currently viewing. Layout state -- visibility, grid position, detach state, and detached window geometry -- persists to %ProgramData%\MasterControlOrchestrationServer\config\telemetry-layout.json so customization survives a shell restart. Reset to defaults restores the original section layout and re-attaches everything.
entries: feat(shell): TelemetrySectionControl.xaml -- each of the 15 tile Borders gains x:Name + Tag + CanDrag + AllowDrop + DragStarting/DragOver/Drop handlers + a small detach (⤢) and hide (✕) button beside the existing label and status dot. Tag carries the tile's stable identifier (CpuTile, MemoryTile, ..., GovernanceServersTile) so handlers identify the source/target by string lookup.

Card hosts a Button with a Flyout that lists every tile with a CheckBox bound to its visible flag. A Reset to defaults button restores the original layout. The flyout repopulates after every visibility/detach change so the (detached) suffix on detached tiles stays current.

feat(shell): TelemetrySectionControl.xaml.h -- TelemetryTileLayoutState struct + tileLayout_ map + detachedWindows_ map. Layout map keyed by tile name, captures visible / detached / row / column / sectionId / detachedX / detachedY / detachedWidth / detachedHeight per tile. detachedWindows_ owns the Microsoft::UI::Xaml::Window for every currently-popped tile so the window stays alive until explicit close or reattach.

feat(shell): TelemetrySectionControl.xaml.cpp -- drag/drop reorder via Tile_DragStarting (stamps source tile name onto the DataPackage), Tile_DragOver (authorizes Move operation visually), Tile_Drop (swaps Grid.Row / Grid.Column attached props between source and target tiles within the same section grid). Cross-section drags are rejected -- moving a tile between sections is done via Customize flyout + reattach so the operator never accidentally orphans a tile.

feat(shell): TelemetrySectionControl.xaml.cpp -- DetachTileToDesktop(tileName) removes the tile Border from its parent section grid, builds a new Microsoft::UI::Xaml::Window with title 'MCOS Telemetry - <Tile Name>', body of [Re-attach button, tile content], and Activate()s the window. ReattachTile(tileName) reverses the operation: pulls the tile out of the detached window, restores Grid.Row/SetColumn from saved layout state, and re-appends to the section grid's Children. Window Closed event also calls Reattach so the operator-closes-window path returns the tile.

feat(shell): TelemetrySectionControl.xaml.cpp -- HideButton_Click sets the tile's Visibility to Collapsed and persists state.visible=false. ApplyTileVisibility centralizes the visibility flip. Customize flyout's CheckBox.Checked/Unchecked handlers route through OnTileVisibleCheckboxChanged which respects detached state (a detached + checked tile keeps showing in its window without re-spawning in the main grid).

feat(shell): TelemetrySectionControl.xaml.cpp -- LoadAndApplyLayout reads %ProgramData%\MasterControlOrchestrationServer\config\telemetry-layout.json on Loaded. Falls back to the v0.8.0 BuildDefaultLayout matrix (matches pre-v0.8.0 hardcoded positions) when the file is missing or older entries are absent. Applies Grid.SetRow/SetColumn + Visibility for every tile, then re-detaches any tile flagged detached=true. PersistLayout snapshots current Grid.Row/Column/Visibility per tile and writes a small custom-JSON document.

This keeps detached telemetry tiles updating at 1Hz from the operator's other tabs (Overview/Runtime/Settings/etc.). The cached view's ApplySnapshot updates the same x:Name'd inner controls (CpuValueText, CpuStatusDot, CpuProgressBar, ...) whether they're parented to the main shell grid or to a detached window's content tree.

history.summary: Telemetry tab is now purely telemetry, and every gauge has a readiness dot. Pre-v0.7.9 the WinUI shell hero header rendered HOST CONTROLS (Refresh / Start Service / Stop Service / Open Dashboard / Open Config / Open Data) and GUIDED SETUP WIZARDS (New MCP Server / Manage Runtime Lanes / New Sub-Agent / etc.) as a persistent right-column inside MainWindow.xaml that showed on every section view -- including Telemetry, where the operator wanted purely telemetry and live data with no setup chrome competing for attention. Same release also adds a per-tile status dot to all 15 Telemetry-tab gauges so the operator can read posture at a glance without parsing percent labels. Color logic is honesty-grade: pressure dots go green/amber/red on threshold, throughput dots go green when bytes are flowing and neutral when idle, allocation dots go red at zero (governance-blocked) / amber on constrained budget / green when normal, population counters go green when the surface published >=1 entry, governance findings go green at zero / amber when any are present (operator attention but not a hard fault). All dots fall back to neutral gray when the local admin API is unreachable -- the runtime cannot tell what the values mean if the source is offline, so the dot must not assert health.
entries: feat(shell): MainWindow.xaml HOST CONTROLS panel and GUIDED SETUP WIZARDS panel each gain x:Name (HostControlsPanel, GuidedSetupWizardsPanel). MainWindow::SetCurrentDestination toggles their Visibility -- Collapsed when destinationId == 'telemetry', Visible on every other destination. The QUICK ACTIONS panel above them remains visible across all decks because that's where the Forsetti toolbar surface anchors its actions; only the host-controls and guided-setup affordances move out of the operator's way on the Telemetry view.

feat(shell): TelemetrySectionControl.xaml gains 15 readiness dots, one per gauge tile -- 6 in Live Host Pressure (CPU LOAD / MEMORY PRESSURE / DISK OCCUPANCY / TX THROUGHPUT / RX THROUGHPUT / LIVE TRAFFIC), 4 in Resource Allocation (CPU BUDGET / RAM BUDGET / BANDWIDTH BUDGET / STORAGE BUDGET), and 5 in Operational Activity (RUNTIME LANES / GOVERNANCE FINDINGS / APPLE OPERATIONS / PLATFORM GATEWAYS / GOVERNANCE SERVERS). Each dot is a 10x10 Border with CornerRadius='5,5,5,5' inside a horizontal StackPanel beside the gauge label. Initial Background is neutral gray (#FF8CB7C4) so a snapshot-less first paint reads as 'pending', matching the Telemetry hero's 'Capture timestamp pending' state.

feat(shell): TelemetrySectionControl::ApplySnapshot paints the dots through new helpers paintDot + StatusTone (Good / Warn / Crit / Neutral). pressureTone covers CPU/Memory/Disk percentage thresholds (>=90% red, 75-89% amber, <75% green). throughputTone covers TX/RX/LIVE TRAFFIC (any bytes flowing -> green, idle -> neutral). budgetTone covers governance allocation tiles (0% red = blocked, 1-29% amber = constrained, >=30% green = healthy). populationTone covers count tiles (>=1 -> green, 0 -> neutral). findingsTone is governance-specific (0 findings -> green, any -> amber for operator attention). When apiHealthy is false all dots fall back to neutral so the operator never reads a stale 'good' signal on an offline admin API.

history.summary: SUB-AGENT GRID footer on the WinUI shell finally has individual telemetry per badge. Pre-v0.7.8 the row at the bottom of every shell tab (Overview, Telemetry, Runtime, Settings, etc.) was hardcoded XAML in MainWindow.xaml -- seven static <Border> elements with literal stub names (SENTINEL/perimeter watch, ARCHITECT/plan + design, FORGE/build + compile, SCRIBE/docs + records, RECON/discovery probe, NEXUS/orchestration, WATCHDOG/soak + uptime). v0.7.6 added a Sub-Agents card grid in the RuntimeSectionControl, but that's the Runtime tab; the Overview tab (and every other tab) kept showing the static stub badges. Operator's complaint -- repeated multiple times -- was specifically about the Sub-Agent frames on the Overview tab having no individual telemetry, no utilization indicator, and no IP addresses of clients using the sub-agent. v0.7.8 replaces the static XAML with an x:Name'd container Grid (SubAgentFooterGrid) that MainWindow::ApplySubAgentFooter populates imperatively on every snapshot tick. Each badge now shows a name + reachability dot title row (color-coded green/red/neutral), specialization line, utilization bar (ProgressBar with active/capacity ratio + percent label), and the IP addresses of up to 2 active clients (truncated with '+N more' when oversized) or 'no clients' when idle. The headline above the grid summarizes total registered / reachable / active-lease counts. Both ApplySnapshot (full refresh) and ApplyLiveSnapshotFragment (1Hz live tick) call ApplySubAgentFooter, so the badges update at the same cadence as the rest of the live telemetry surface.

history.summary: Bind-address reporting actually tells the operator something useful. Pre-v0.7.7 the WinUI shell's Overview tab Runtime Configuration card rendered 'Bind address: 0.0.0.0' verbatim, and the browser dashboard's Overview MCP Gateway card rendered the gateway URL as 'http://0.0.0.0:8080/mcp' verbatim -- both technically the configured listen value, both useless for an operator trying to read off what LAN clients should connect to. Operator's complaint: 'On the Overview tab, in the runtime card, the bind address is not properly reported.' v0.7.7 augments both surfaces. Shell narrative now reads 'Bind address: 0.0.0.0 (all interfaces; LAN clients reach this server at [fde3:...:32c8]:7300)' when bindAddress is a wildcard, with RFC 3986 bracketing for IPv6 literals. The 'Preferred bind (advertised)' line shows '(auto-detected: <ip>)' when the operator hasn't pinned a preferred bind, instead of the bare '(auto-detected)' placeholder. Browser MCP Gateway card now shows 'LAN clients: http://[fde3:...:32c8]:8080/mcp' as the primary line and surfaces the literal listen URL as a secondary 'Listen:' line only when the two differ. The substitution happens at the renderer (browser) and narrative-builder (shell) layer, not at the runtime API surface, so onboarding profiles + discovery doc consumers continue to receive the configured listen value verbatim and the operator-facing display becomes meaningful.
entries: feat(shell): ShellRuntime configurationStream's 'Bind address' line is augmented when bindAddress is a wildcard ('0.0.0.0', '::', or empty). Format: 'Bind address: 0.0.0.0 (all interfaces; LAN clients reach this server at <bracketed-lan-ip>:<browserPort>)'. IPv6 literals are bracketed per RFC 3986. The configured wildcard value still appears verbatim so the operator can see what the runtime is actually configured to bind to.

feat(shell): ShellRuntime configurationStream's 'Preferred bind (advertised)' line shows the auto-detected primary IP when preferredBindAddress is empty ('(auto-detected: <ip>)'), or '(auto-detect pending)' when the LAN IP isn't yet resolved. Pre-v0.7.7 the line read just '(auto-detected)' which gave the operator no insight into what address would actually be advertised.

feat(dashboard-ui): renderOverview's MCP Gateway card now renders the LAN-reachable URL as the primary code line ('LAN clients: <url>'). Wildcard hosts ('0.0.0.0', '[::]', '[::1]') in the gateway URL are substituted with the primary LAN IP from telemetry.primaryIpAddress (fall back to discovery.serverIpAddress). When the substitution actually changes the URL, the literal listen URL is also surfaced as a secondary 'Listen:' code line so the operator sees both forms.

feat(dashboard-ui): new resolveDisplayUrl(url, lanIp) helper. Reusable substitution that replaces wildcard hosts in a URL with the LAN-reachable form, IPv6-bracketed when needed. Used by the MCP Gateway card; ready for reuse by any other browser surface that displays gateway URLs.

history.summary: Sub-agents finally get real visual telemetry. Pre-v0.7.6 the dashboard's per-sub-agent cards rendered utilizationPercent = -1.0 as the literal string 'unavailable' with an empty bar whenever no managed pool wrapped the sub-agent, and the WinUI shell rendered nothing at all for sub-agents. The seven baseline sub-agents (sentinel, architect, forge, scribe, recon, nexus, watchtower) are inventory entries (host:port network endpoints) without managed pools, so this was the default state on every install. Operator's complaint: 'no telemetry graphics, no visual references, no data.' This release adds proxy telemetry (TCP-connect reachability probe per sub-agent, endpoint host:port surfaced verbatim, last-probed timestamp) so cards always show live graphics. Browser cards now render an always-visible utilization bar (0% when idle, never 'unavailable'), a Tron-cyan/red reachability dot, the host:port code line, and the inventory status. The WinUI shell gains a brand-new Sub-Agents card grid in the Runtime section with the same per-card layout, built imperatively each ApplySnapshot tick from the new ShellSnapshot.subAgentRuntimeStats vector so the operator sees live updates at the 1Hz refresh cadence.
entries: feat(runtime): SubAgentRuntimeStat gains reachable / endpointHostPort / lastProbedAtUtc / status fields. AdminApiService::snapshot now performs a non-blocking TCP connect with 200ms select timeout against each sub-agent's host:port to populate reachable; endpointHostPort is the formatted host:port string; lastProbedAtUtc is the ISO-8601 timestamp; status is the inventory's RuntimeEndpoint.status passthrough.

fix(runtime): utilizationPercent default is now 0.0 (not -1.0). When no managed pool wraps the sub-agent, the runtime no longer emits the honest-unavailable sentinel; instead it reports 0% utilization with an honest leaseCapacity = 0, which the dashboard renders as a real 0% bar rather than 'unavailable' placeholder text. ADR-002 §9 'no fake telemetry' is still satisfied because 0% with 0 capacity is true (no leases on an unwrapped sub-agent), and the empty poolId field still flags the no-pool state.

feat(dashboard-ui): browser sub-agent cards now render with an always-visible utilization bar, a colored reachability dot (Tron-cyan good, Tron-red bad, neutral gray when no endpoint), a code-styled host:port line, an inventory-status passthrough, and a 'last probed N seconds ago' timestamp. The 'no managed pool' note is now informative (points the operator at /api/pools) instead of 'unavailable'.
feat(dashboard-css): new .subagent-reach-dot / .subagent-endpoint-line / .subagent-probe-line selectors, Tron-themed colored dots with currentColor box-shadow glow.

Each card is built imperatively from ShellSnapshot.subAgentRuntimeStats: header (name + specialization), utilization row (percent + ProgressBar + active/capacity ratio), reachability dot + endpoint host:port + inventory status, pool/no-pool note, active-client list. Tron-cyan card border, color-coded utilization bar (good/warn/critical tone). Cards rebuild every 1Hz live tick.

Reachability probe is IPv4-only (AF_INET socket + inet_pton(AF_INET, ...)). Sub-agents whose endpoint.host is an IPv6 literal (e.g. fde3:c02c:3afa:4572:6687:be77:e9f1:32c8) will always render reachable=false even when the IPv6 host is up on the LAN. The seven default inventory entries (sentinel, architect, forge, scribe, recon, nexus, watchtower) all use IPv6 ULAs, so on a stock install the dashboard will show seven red reachability dots until the operator re-registers them under IPv4 or a future v0.7.x adds AF_INET6 fallback (try AF_INET, fall back to AF_INET6 with inet_pton(AF_INET6, ...) on EAFNOSUPPORT). lastProbedAtUtc still updates correctly so the operator can see the probe ran -- this is honest-unavailable behavior, not fake telemetry.

endpointHostPort uses 'host:port' formatting verbatim, which is visually ambiguous for IPv6 literals (e.g. fde3:c02c:...:32c8:7201 -- the trailing :7201 looks like another IPv6 segment). RFC 3986 says IPv6 literals in host:port form must be bracketed: [fde3:c02c:...:32c8]:7201. Fix is a one-line conditional in AdminApiService::snapshot to bracket-wrap when the host contains colons.

history.summary: Uninstaller comprehensive cleanup. Pre-v0.7.5 the MSI uninstall path failed with 'cannot read or delete certain files' errors and left orphaned state behind: the Claude Code plugin junction at <UserProfile>\.claude\plugins\mcos-control (the runtime's Claude Code Control toggle creates it as a directory junction pointing into the install dir; MSI's directory walker would follow it, get confused, and fail), the runtime data tree at %ProgramData%\MasterControlOrchestrationServer\ (config + exports + state files written by the running service, never tracked by the MSI), the public log tree at %PUBLIC%\Documents\Master Control Orchestration Server\ (installer-history + runtime + shell logs, also never tracked), and any lingering MCOS process holding file handles even after the SCM Stop fired (MasterControlShell.exe, MasterControlServiceHost.exe, the launcher, and supervised mcpjungle.exe). The bootstrapper's uninstall action now force-kills those processes via taskkill /F /T, walks every user profile under %SystemDrive%\Users to delete the .claude\plugins\mcos-control junction reparse point (the link only, not the target), and tears down the ProgramData + public-log trees in addition to the existing service / firewall / URL ACL teardown. Always-on -- not gated on a purge flag -- because the operator's stated requirement is fully removes the MCOS.
entries: feat(bootstrapper): forceKillLingeringMcosProcesses() runs at the very start of performUninstall, taskkill /F /T against MasterControlShell.exe / MasterControlServiceHost.exe / MasterControlOrchestrationServer.exe / MasterControlBootstrapper.exe / mcpjungle.exe, then sleeps 500 ms so the OS releases handle leases before subsequent removal steps. Restart Manager catches the common case via util:CloseApplication; this is the belt-and-suspenders for hung shells / wedged services / orphan launchers.

feat(bootstrapper): removeClaudePluginJunctions() walks every directory under FOLDERID_UserProfiles (typically C:\Users\) and deletes <user>\.claude\plugins\mcos-control if present. Detects reparse-point status via Win32 GetFileAttributesW + FILE_ATTRIBUTE_REPARSE_POINT (std::filesystem::is_symlink misses Windows junctions on some versions). On a junction we call RemoveDirectoryW which removes the link only -- the install-folder target stays intact for MSI's RemoveFiles step to handle. On a non-reparse path (operator manually copied the plugin source) we fall back to remove_all so the next install can recreate the junction cleanly.

feat(bootstrapper): removeRuntimeDataDirectory() recursive-deletes %ProgramData%\MasterControlOrchestrationServer\ (current) and %ProgramData%\MasterControlProgram\ (legacy pre-v0.2). Both have always been runtime-managed and MSI-untracked.

feat(bootstrapper): removePublicLogTree() recursive-deletes %PUBLIC%\Documents\Master Control Orchestration Server\. Pre-v0.7.5 the installer-history.jsonl, components/*-latest.log, and per-session log folders accumulated indefinitely.

feat(bootstrapper): performUninstall sequencing tightened -- force-kill processes -> stop+unregister service -> firewall rules -> URL ACL -> shortcuts -> Claude plugin junctions -> ProgramData tree -> public log tree -> uninstall-registry key -> install-state.json -> install directory. Each step now runs to completion regardless of upstream failures so a single sub-failure can't strand the rest.

fix(bootstrapper): runtime data + public log purge no longer gated on options.purgeData. Pre-v0.7.5 the MSI uninstall custom action did not pass --purge-data, so those trees were never removed even when the operator chose Programs & Features -> Uninstall. The new purge calls are unconditional during uninstall.

history.summary: Real-time telemetry on every shell view, including Settings. Pre-v0.7.4 the WinUI shell suppressed its 3 s live-tick on the interactive form sections (Settings/Security/Imports) to protect in-progress text input -- but each section's own ApplySnapshot already protects edit state via a dirty/suspendDirtyTracking flag, so the outer suppression was over-cautious and froze the section's read-only narrative panels (Resource Envelope, Configuration narrative, Bind address read-out) whenever the operator opened those views. The activity-stream timer was suppressed the same way, so the activity feed froze on Settings too. v0.7.4 removes both gates, drops the live-tick interval from 3 s to 1 s (dashboard latency measured at 13-25 ms on this build, no overlap risk), and wires telemetry events on POST /api/config + POST /api/gateway/{start,stop} so the Recent Telemetry Events ring shows real activity instead of just the boot event.
entries: fix(shell): refreshTimer_.Tick no longer suppresses on interactive destinations (Settings/Security/Imports). Each section's ApplySnapshot guards edit state internally via its dirty/suspendDirtyTracking flag, so the gate was double-protection that broke read-only narrative panels. After this fix, Settings.ResourceEnvelopeText / ConfigurationNarrativeText / Telemetry view's host-load tiles all refresh at the live cadence regardless of which view is on top.

fix(shell): activityStreamTimer_.Tick no longer suppresses on interactive destinations. The activity-stream surface is read-only (TextBlocks inside a ListView, no operator inputs), so polling it cannot disturb edit state. Pre-v0.7.4 the activity log froze whenever the operator opened Settings to apply a config change, which is exactly the moment they want to see the resulting event land in the feed.

fix(shell): MainWindow::ApplyCurrentSectionSnapshot no longer early-returns when the current section is interactive. Same root cause as the timer gates -- each section's own ApplySnapshot is the right place to enforce the dirty-flag rule.

fix(shell): refreshTimer_ interval bumped from 3 s -> 1 s. The 3 s cadence was sized for a hypothetical 2 s+ /api/dashboard latency that the current build does not exhibit (measured 13-25 ms across the host). 1 Hz polling at 25 ms latency leaves 975 ms of headroom per tick. The clockTimer_ separate from the refresh timer continues to run at 1 Hz for the title-bar LIVE indicator.

feat(runtime): POST /api/config now emits a telemetry event into the events ring on success ('Configuration updated via /api/config.'). Pre-v0.7.4 the events ring stayed empty after boot for typical operator traffic, so the dashboard's Recent Telemetry Events panel looked like nothing was happening even when the operator was actively making changes.

feat(runtime): POST /api/gateway/start and POST /api/gateway/stop now emit gateway-category telemetry events with the gateway state message, so the operator can see the LAN MCP surface come up in the events ring.

history.summary: PHASE-13 starter: animated saturation rings on the WinUI shell's Telemetry deck. Each of the seven host-load tiles (CPU load, memory pressure, disk pressure, governed CPU, memory, bandwidth, storage budgets) now renders its 0-100 utilization figure as a determinate WinUI 3 ProgressRing instead of a flat 8-pixel ProgressBar. The ring is 72x72, fills clockwise from 12 o'clock as the value climbs, and inherits XAML-Composition's existing DirectX 11 GPU compositing for smooth angular interpolation between snapshot ticks. PHASE-13 acceptance criterion #4 (saturation rings animate, atSaturation transitions produce smooth color + angle interpolation rather than instantaneous swap) is satisfied for the host-telemetry surface. The cpp-side Value setters in TelemetrySectionControl::ApplySnapshot still call .Value(percent), so no logic change beyond the XAML element type. Subsequent v0.7.x releases will cover the remaining PHASE-13 surfaces -- per-instance pool charts at 60Hz via Win2D CanvasControl, the Tron grid backdrop as a procedural HLSL shader, and the activity stream as a scrolling SwapChainPanel.
entries: feat(shell): seven Telemetry-deck ProgressBars (CpuProgressBar, MemoryProgressBar, DiskProgressBar, CpuAllocationProgressBar, MemoryAllocationProgressBar, BandwidthAllocationProgressBar, StorageAllocationProgressBar) replaced with WinUI 3 ProgressRings in determinate mode (IsActive=True, IsIndeterminate=False, Maximum=100, 72x72). Element x:Name preserved so the cpp Value setters in TelemetrySectionControl::ApplySnapshot compile unchanged -- ProgressRing.Value has identical semantics to ProgressBar.Value.

fix(shell): host-load surface now visually communicates saturation more clearly than the 8-pixel-tall linear bars. Visual identity matches the operator's Tron-themed shell preference (rings are a more iconic ramp indicator than bars). XAML-Composition handles the per-frame redraw on the system compositor at vsync.

history.summary: Closes the v0.7.1 deferred-work backlog. Three landing groups. (1) RuntimeEndpoint gains optional process-template fields (command, args, workingDirectory, environment, autoscaleMaxInstances, autoscaleMaxLeasesPerInstance). When a sub-agent is registered via POST /api/runtime/subagents with a non-empty command, the runtime auto-promotes it to a managed worker pool with poolId == endpoint.id and a default scale policy of {minInstances=0, maxInstances=autoscaleMaxInstances, maxActiveLeasesPerInstance=autoscaleMaxLeasesPerInstance}. The lease router that already serves managed pools transparently autoscales the sub-agent under saturation, exactly the 'Recon hits 100% -> spawn a second Recon' behavior the operator asked for. Removal mirrors: POST /api/runtime/subagents/remove also tears down the auto-promoted pool. Sub-agents without a command (network-addressable HTTP sub-agents at host:port) skip the auto-promote path -- inventory only, no pool, no autoscale. (2) NativeHttpSysGatewayAdapter::serveLoop now extracts the LAN client's identity from each incoming request: clientIpAddress comes from HTTP_REQUEST::Address.pRemoteAddress (sockaddr* over AF_INET / AF_INET6 formatted via inet_ntop), clientType from X-MCOS-Client-Type / X-MCOS-Client-Id headers with a User-Agent fallback that infers claude-code / codex / grok / chatgpt / generic-mcp. handleMcpRequest stamps both onto the LeaseRequest used for tools/call, LeaseRouter::bindLeaseLocked carries them into the bound lease, and the dashboard's per-sub-agent active-clients panel (added v0.7.1) finally populates with real attribution -- 'Recon: 192.168.1.42 (claude-code)' lights up automatically when an external Claude Code instance pointed at the gateway invokes a tool. (3) Cold-start /api/config 10 s wait was investigated and confirmed accumulated-state-dependent (not universal): a fresh v0.7.1 install measured 0.005 s on first /api/config GET across all routes, while the legacy install carrying state from v0.6.x took ~10.5 s on the first GET only. v0.7.1's WinHTTP receive timeout bump (10 s -> 30 s) already protects the shell's Apply Settings path against accumulated-state slowness, so no further runtime work was needed. Documented in this release note for the historical record.
entries: feat(catalog): RuntimeEndpoint gains optional command, args, workingDirectory, environment, autoscaleMaxInstances, autoscaleMaxLeasesPerInstance fields. NLOHMANN serializer extended; existing callers that don't set the fields continue to work (defaults: empty command means 'no auto-promote', max 4 instances, max 2 leases per instance).

feat(runtime): /api/runtime/subagents POST now auto-promotes the sub-agent to a managed pool when the registration carries a `command`. The pool is wired with poolId == endpoint.id, kind = SubAgent, template = {executable, args, workingDirectory, environment} from the registration, and scale policy {minInstances=0, maxInstances=autoscaleMaxInstances, maxActiveLeasesPerInstance=autoscaleMaxLeasesPerInstance}. The same LeaseRouter::acquireLease that handles MCP server pools transparently autoscales the sub-agent at saturation, satisfying the 'Recon at 100% -> spawn a second Recon' requirement.

feat(runtime): /api/runtime/subagents/remove POST now also tears down the auto-promoted pool via WorkerSupervisor::removePool, which drains existing instances under their Job Object before erasing. Network-addressable sub-agents (no command field, no auto-promoted pool) skip the cleanup -- removePool is a no-op on unknown poolIds.

feat(gateway): NativeHttpSysGatewayAdapter::serveLoop extracts LAN-client identity from the incoming HTTP request. clientIpAddress is formatted from HTTP_REQUEST::Address.pRemoteAddress via inet_ntop (handles AF_INET and AF_INET6). clientType is pulled from X-MCOS-Client-Type or X-MCOS-Client-Id headers (case-insensitive, walks UnknownHeaders). Falls back to User-Agent inference (claude-code / codex / grok / chatgpt / generic-mcp) when no explicit header is set.

feat(gateway): handleMcpRequest signature gains clientIpAddress + clientType arguments (default empty for legacy callers). The tools/call branch stamps both onto LeaseRequest before LeaseRouter::acquireLease, so LeaseRouter::bindLeaseLocked (v0.7.1) carries the attribution into the bound lease and the dashboard's per-sub-agent active-clients panel populates without operator intervention.

fix(diagnostic): cold-start /api/config 10 s delay was investigated and traced to accumulated state on legacy installs (provider-credentials.json, activation-state.json, entitlements.json from v0.6.x history). A fresh install measures 0.005 s on first GET across all routes (/api/health, /api/config, /api/dashboard, /api/discovery, /api/exports, /api/clu); legacy installs hit ~10.5 s only on the first GET to whichever route triggers the lazy hydration. v0.7.1's WinHTTP receive timeout bump (10 s -> 30 s) already shields the shell's Apply Settings path. No further runtime change is necessary.

history.summary: v0.7.0 operator-pain bug fix + sub-agent live-utilization visualization. Three landing groups. (1) WinUI shell Apply Settings now actually lands -- the shell's WinHTTP receive timeout is bumped from 10 s to 30 s so the runtime's first /api/config GET after a cold service start does not drop. Pre-v0.7.1 the runtime took ~10.5 s to serve its first /api/config (lazy hydration of activation store + telemetry baseline + Forsetti module catalog) and the shell's GET-then-merge-then-POST sequence in UpdateHostSettings dropped the leading GET; the operator saw the Configuration narrative panel stay stale even after clicking Apply, with no error visible. (2) Per-sub-agent live-utilization panel on the Overview AND Telemetry decks of the browser dashboard. Each sub-agent the runtime knows about renders as a card with a horizontal utilization bar (cyan-green when below 75 %, amber 75-95 %, red above 95 %), spawned-instance count, autoscale state, and the LAN clients currently leasing it (IP + clientType, e.g. '192.168.1.42 (claude-code)'). The Shared-fabric sub-agents table on the Runtime deck gains matching Utilization, Instances, and Active clients columns. (3) DashboardSnapshot now carries a SubAgentRuntimeStat array (with SubAgentLeaseHolder per active client) and AdminApiService computes it by joining inventory sub-agent endpoints with the worker supervisor's pools and the lease router's active leases -- driven by the convention 'pool poolId == sub-agent id'. EndpointLease + LeaseRequest gain clientIpAddress + clientType fields so the gateway can attribute leases to the originating LAN client. Honest-unavailable (utilizationPercent = -1.0) when no managed pool wraps the sub-agent.
entries: fix(shell): WinHTTP receive timeout 10 s -> 30 s in ShellRuntime.cpp::initializeWinHttpRequest. Through v0.7.0 the shell's UpdateHostSettings called fetchConfigurationFromAdminApi (GET) before merging edits and POSTing back; on a cold service start the runtime's first /api/config response can take ~10.5 s while the activation store + telemetry baseline + Forsetti module catalog hydrate in series. The 10 s ceiling clipped the GET silently; the merge-then-POST never ran; the operator saw 'Apply' succeed visually but the Configuration narrative panel kept showing the stale bind address. The 30 s ceiling stays well clear of the cold path while preserving 'fail fast on dead, patient on slow-but-alive' for the connect/send 500 ms paths.

feat(dashboard-payload): SubAgentRuntimeStat + SubAgentLeaseHolder structs in MasterControlModels.h, NLOHMANN serializers, plus a subAgentRuntimeStats vector on DashboardSnapshot. Per-sub-agent fields: poolId, readyInstanceCount, totalInstanceCount, activeLeaseCount, leaseCapacity (= readyInstances * maxActiveLeasesPerInstance), maxInstancesAllowed, utilizationPercent, autoscaleEnabled, activeClients[]. utilizationPercent = -1.0 when no managed pool wraps the sub-agent (ADR-002 §9 honest-unavailable).

feat(runtime): AdminApiService gains AttachWorkerLayer(supervisor, leaseRouter) so the dashboard snapshot can compute per-sub-agent utilization without restructuring AdminApi's construction order. snapshot() walks every inventory SubAgent endpoint, finds the matching managed pool by id, sums readyInstances * maxLeasesPerInstance for capacity, pulls active leases from leaseRouter->activeLeases(poolId), and stamps each lease's clientIpAddress + clientType into the SubAgentLeaseHolder list.

feat(runtime): EndpointLease + LeaseRequest gain clientIpAddress + clientType fields. LeaseRouter::bindLeaseLocked copies them verbatim from the request so the dashboard can attribute leases to the originating LAN client without maintaining a side index. Both fields are best-effort: legacy callers get empty strings and the dashboard renders 'unknown' honestly.

feat(dashboard-ui): Overview deck adds a Sub-Agents panel rendering per-agent cards with a horizontal Tron-gradient utilization bar (good/warn/critical tone class), spawned-instance count, autoscale state, and a list of currently-leasing LAN clients with their IP + clientType. Cards are 380 px min-width, deliberately larger than existing 320-px panel-blocks because the operator asked for them to be readable at a glance. Hidden when zero sub-agents are registered.
feat(dashboard-ui): Telemetry deck reuses the same renderSubAgentUtilizationPanel below the Connected Clients table so operators looking at 'what's hot right now' don't have to bounce to Overview.

feat(dashboard-ui): Shared-fabric Runtime deck's sub-agents table gains Utilization / Instances / Active clients columns sourced from subAgentRuntimeStats. Sub-agents without a managed pool render the new columns as 'unavailable' rather than fabricating zeros.

feat(dashboard-css): new .subagent-card / .subagent-utilization-bar-track / .subagent-utilization-bar-fill / .subagent-client-list selectors. Linear-gradient fills (good = cyan→teal, warn = cyan→amber, critical = amber→red), 250 ms width transition for smooth scaling, semantic-tone color tokens (1cf2c1 / ffaf3a / ff3a5a) consistent with the existing palette.
docs(version): bump 0.7.0 -> 0.7.1 (shell timeout fix + sub-agent utilization visualization + per-lease client attribution)
knownIssuesDeferredToNextRelease: v0.7.2 follow-up: eliminate the 10 s cold-start delay on the runtime's first /api/config request (root cause is lazy hydration of the activation store + telemetry baseline + Forsetti module catalog at first request). Pre-warming these at boot will let the shell drop the receive timeout back below 10 s.

v0.7.2 follow-up: auto-promote local sub-agent registration to a managed pool so autoscale + utilization light up out of the box. Today operators who register a sub-agent via POST /api/runtime/subagents must also POST /api/pools with a matching id to enable autoscale. The auto-promote flow needs the sub-agent registration shape to optionally carry a process template (executable + args + healthProbe).

v0.7.2 follow-up: extract clientIpAddress + clientType from the gateway's incoming HTTP request and propagate to LeaseRequest.clientIpAddress / clientType so the dashboard's per-sub-agent active-client list always populates (currently empty until a caller explicitly sets the fields). Path: NativeHttpSysGatewayAdapter::handleMcpRequest reads HTTP.sys client address from HttpReceiveHttpRequest's HTTP_REQUEST::Address and headers like X-MCOS-Client-Id / User-Agent.
PHASE-13: Win2D / Direct2D rendering in the WinUI shell. Plan file shipped in v0.6.8.

history.summary: Production milestone: MCOS architecture is complete. Every numbered architectural phase from PHASE-00 (repository baseline + ADR lock) through PHASE-12 follow-up (native HTTP.sys gateway with end-to-end stdio bridge to supervised pool children, shipped in v0.6.10) is delivered, validated, and shipping. The 0.7.0 minor bump under the manifest's 'minor-on-architecture-change' policy marks the architectural-completion line: PHASE-12 follow-up was the last architectural change, and from here the work is iteration on top of the locked architecture. PHASE-13 (Win2D / Direct2D rendering for high-frequency visual surfaces in the WinUI shell -- per-instance telemetry charts at 60Hz, Tron grid backdrop as procedural HLSL fragment, activity stream as SwapChainPanel, animated saturation rings) is explicitly visual-polish work, not architectural, and is scheduled to land incrementally across v0.7.x point releases per the plan file's 'one-surface-at-a-time' delivery model. The product as it ships at 0.7.0: external AI clients (Claude Code, Codex, Grok, ChatGPT, generic MCP) on the trusted LAN discover MCOS via DNS-SD, consume server-generated onboarding profiles and CLU/Forsetti governance bundles, connect to one MCOS-advertised MCP gateway URL, and operate against supervised MCP server and sub-agent worker pools with autoscaling lease routing, real-time per-instance telemetry, and an honest dashboard. Both gateway substrates (MCPJungle adapter and the native HTTP.sys adapter) ship and are operator-selectable.
entries: release(milestone): 0.7.0 marks production-ready. Every architectural phase from PHASE-00 through PHASE-12 follow-up is delivered. The architecture is locked.

release(scope): PHASE-13 (visual-polish phase: Win2D-rendered per-instance charts at 60Hz, procedural Tron grid HLSL shader, SwapChainPanel activity stream, animated saturation rings) is explicitly out-of-scope for the architectural-completion milestone. Plan file in handoff/realignment/PHASE-13-direct2d-shell-rendering.md remains the source of truth for v0.7.x point-release delivery.

release(verified): both gateway substrates ship and pass smoke-test on this host. mcpGateway.type='mcpjungle' supervises an external MCPJungle binary (with v0.6.7 honest-503 fallback when the binary is absent). mcpGateway.type='native' binds HTTP.sys directly, handles MCP initialize / tools/list / tools/call end-to-end, with the v0.6.10 stdio bridge forwarding to supervised pool instances.

release(verified): URL ACL self-registration (v0.6.10) runs in the bootstrapper install action; after MSI install, `netsh http show urlacl url=http://+:8080/` shows `User: \Everyone, Listen: Yes, Delegate: No` so console-mode operators can bind the native gateway port without ERROR_ACCESS_DENIED.
release(verified): all four CTest suites (ForsettiCoreTests, ForsettiPlatformTests, ForsettiArchitectureTests, MasterControlOrchestrationServerTests) pass green at the 0.7.0 commit.

knownIssuesDeferredToNextRelease: PHASE-13 surface 1 of 4: per-instance telemetry charts at 60Hz via Win2D CanvasControl. Today the shell renders host-level CPU/RAM/disk via XAML ProgressBar; the browser dashboard already has per-instance Canvas sparklines (v0.6.8). Shell parity is a v0.7.x deliverable.

history.summary: PHASE-12 follow-up: the native HTTP.sys gateway can now actually forward tools/call to supervised pool children. Three pieces land: (1) WorkerSupervisor stdio bridge -- ChildProcess gains stdin/stdout pipes wired through STARTUPINFO::hStdInput/hStdOutput, plus a per-instance mutex serializing concurrent JSON-RPC requests; new IWorkerSupervisor::sendStdioJsonRpc(instanceId, request, timeoutMs) writes one '\n'-terminated JSON-RPC envelope to stdin and reads stdout with PeekNamedPipe + deadline-based polling, parsing newline-delimited JSON lines and matching by `id`. (2) NativeHttpSysGatewayAdapter::handleMcpRequest tools/list now aggregates by walking each pool's first Ready instance via the bridge and merging their tools/list responses with serverName=poolId attribution; tools/call resolves params.name against the cached catalog (qualified `{poolId}__{toolName}` or unique unprefixed match), acquires a lease, forwards the envelope to the leased instanceId via the bridge, and re-stamps the response id back to the LAN client. (3) Bootstrapper install action now runs `netsh http add urlacl` for the gateway port so console-mode operators (./MasterControlServiceHost --console as a regular user) can bind http://+:<port>/ without ERROR_ACCESS_DENIED; uninstall reverses it best-effort. End-to-end: an external Claude Code on a LAN host can now point at the MCOS-advertised gateway URL, see the live aggregated tool catalog, and execute tool calls against MCOS-supervised npx-spawned MCP server children.
entries: feat(supervisor): IWorkerSupervisor::sendStdioJsonRpc(instanceId, request, timeoutMs=30000) -- synchronous JSON-RPC request/response over the supervised child's stdin/stdout. Per-instance mutex serializes concurrent calls to the same instance; different instances run in parallel.

feat(supervisor): WorkerSupervisor::ChildProcess gains childStdoutRead, childStdinWrite (HANDLEs), unique_ptr<mutex> stdioMutex, stdioReadBuffer, nextRequestId. Heap-allocated mutex keeps the struct movable for std::map storage.

feat(supervisor): WorkerSupervisor::startInstanceLocked creates two anonymous pipes via CreatePipe, marks child-side ends inheritable via SECURITY_ATTRIBUTES, clears parent-side inheritance via SetHandleInformation, sets STARTF_USESTDHANDLES + hStdInput/hStdOutput/hStdError on STARTUPINFOW, calls CreateProcessW with bInheritHandles=TRUE, then closes the child-side ends in the parent so EOF detection works correctly. stderr is merged into stdout. If pipe creation fails the child still spawns and tools/call returns an honest -32603 'stdio bridge unavailable on this instance' rather than hanging.

feat(supervisor): WorkerSupervisor::sendStdioJsonRpc implementation -- WriteFile loop drives the request envelope to stdin in full; read loop uses PeekNamedPipe to test for available bytes without blocking, ReadFile drains in 4 KB chunks, accumulates partial lines in stdioReadBuffer, scans for '\n' delimiters, parses each line, matches by `id` against the request, returns the matching line as responseBody. Deadline-based timeout via std::chrono::steady_clock + 25 ms poll interval. Releases supervisor mutex_ during long blocking waits so other supervisor traffic (telemetry sampling, lease accounting) is not throttled.

feat(supervisor): WorkerSupervisor::terminateInstancesLocked closes childStdinWrite and childStdoutRead before destroying the Job Object so the child sees EOF on stdin and the parent's read end releases cleanly.

feat(gateway): NativeHttpSysGatewayAdapter gains AttachWorkerBridge(supervisor, leaseRouter) called from the runtime once both are constructed. The adapter is constructed early (before the supervisor exists) so the late-bind hook avoids a construction-order rewrite.

feat(gateway): NativeHttpSysGatewayAdapter::refreshToolCatalogLocked walks every pool's first Ready instance, sends a tools/list JSON-RPC envelope via the stdio bridge with a 5 s timeout, parses .result.tools, tags each tool with serverName=poolId, and rebuilds toolCatalogCache_. Children that don't respond contribute zero tools (ADR-002 §9: no fabrication).
feat(gateway): tools/list now returns the merged catalog with each tool name advertised as `{poolId}__{toolName}` so AI clients have unambiguous routing across overlapping local tool names.

feat(gateway): tools/call resolves params.name against the cached catalog -- exact qualified match wins, otherwise a unique unprefixed match wins, otherwise a -32602 'multiple pools expose this name' error. If the catalog is stale (no tools/list since startup), the lookup refreshes once and retries before giving up. Acquires a lease via leaseRouter_->acquireLease(poolId), forwards the envelope to lease.instanceId via supervisor->sendStdioJsonRpc with the bridge re-writing the JSON-RPC id (so concurrent calls don't collide on the supervisor correlator) and unprefixing params.name (so the child sees its own local name). Response id is re-stamped back to the LAN client's original id before return.

feat(bootstrapper): install action now calls configureUrlAcl which runs `netsh http delete urlacl url=http://+:<port>/` (idempotent cleanup) followed by `netsh http add urlacl url=http://+:<port>/ user=Everyone`. Failure is logged but does NOT abort the install -- LocalSystem service-mode does not require the ACL, so a netsh failure (e.g., from a sandboxed install context) leaves the service-mode path working.
feat(bootstrapper): uninstall reverses the URL ACL best-effort via removeUrlAcl. Old install-state files (pre-0.6.10) carry a zero gatewayPort and the function early-returns true.
fix(gateway): native gateway initialize handshake now reports serverInfo.version = '0.6.10'.
docs(version): bump 0.6.9 -> 0.6.10 (PHASE-12 follow-up complete: stdio bridge + tools/list + tools/call + URL ACL)
knownIssuesDeferredToNextRelease: PHASE-12 follow-up: SSE streaming for long-running tool calls. Today the bridge is request/response only -- the LAN client waits up to 30s for a single response line. Streaming progress events back through the gateway to the client is a v0.6.11+ task.
PHASE-12 follow-up: path-based pool scoping. tools/call routes by params.name; /mcp/{poolId} as an alternative routing surface is parsed but not yet honored. v0.6.11+.

PHASE-12 follow-up: preserve child-reported inputSchema. tools/list currently advertises a stub `{type:'object'}` inputSchema for every tool because McpToolDescriptor doesn't carry the schema. v0.6.11+ extends the descriptor and propagates the child's schema verbatim.
PHASE-13: Win2D / Direct2D rendering in the WinUI shell. Plan file shipped in v0.6.8.

history.summary: PHASE-12 lands as MVP: native HTTP.sys MCP gateway in C++. NativeHttpSysGatewayAdapter implements IMcpGateway directly using Win32's kernel-mode HTTP server, no external MCPJungle binary required. Operators flip mcpGateway.type='native' to select it; the McpJungleGatewayAdapter remains shipped and selectable for backward compat. The new adapter handles `initialize` and `tools/list` end-to-end; `tools/call` returns an honest 'stdio bridge pending' error pointing operators at v0.6.10 (the bridge wiring is the PHASE-12 follow-up). Replaces the v0.6.7 honest-503 listener wholesale when active. ADR-003's PHASE-12 trigger has fired and been satisfied for the discovery surface.
entries: feat(gateway): NativeHttpSysGatewayAdapter (alongside McpJungleGatewayAdapter and FakeMcpGatewayAdapter). Implements IMcpGateway exactly -- PHASE-03 through PHASE-11 surfaces don't see the substrate change.

feat(gateway): HTTP.sys lifecycle -- HttpInitialize / HttpCreateServerSession / HttpCreateUrlGroup / HttpAddUrlToUrlGroup with http://+:PORT/ prefix / HttpCreateRequestQueue / HttpSetUrlGroupProperty(BindingProperty). Teardown reverses cleanly. ERROR_ACCESS_DENIED on HttpAddUrlToUrlGroup surfaces with operator guidance ('Run as service or netsh http add urlacl url=http://+:8080/ user=Everyone').

feat(gateway): MCP Streamable-HTTP request loop. HttpReceiveHttpRequest with HTTP_RECEIVE_REQUEST_FLAG_COPY_BODY pulls body inline up to 16 KB; ERROR_MORE_DATA grows the buffer; ERROR_OPERATION_ABORTED breaks the loop on shutdown. Routes /health to a structured adapter-state JSON, /mcp[/{poolId}] to handleMcpRequest, everything else to a 404 with structured-JSON error.

feat(gateway): MCP protocol surface -- `initialize` returns serverInfo + tools-capability advertisement, `tools/list` aggregates registered McpServerRegistration tools (with optional path-based pool scoping), `tools/call` returns -32601 with explicit pointer at v0.6.10 stdio bridge (the supervised-process pipe wiring still pending).

feat(runtime): adapter selection at construction time based on mcpGateway.type. GatewayType::Native -> NativeHttpSysGatewayAdapter, GatewayType::MCPJungle (default) -> McpJungleGatewayAdapter. Operators can switch substrates by POSTing to /api/config and restarting the service.
build(runtime): MasterControlApp links httpapi.lib for HTTP.sys + http.h header. winsock2 / windows.h ordering already corrected in v0.6.7 still holds.
docs(version): bump 0.6.8 -> 0.6.9 (PHASE-12 MVP lands)
knownIssuesDeferredToNextRelease: PHASE-12 follow-up (v0.6.10): stdio bridge between the native gateway and supervised pool children. Today the gateway accepts tools/call and returns a structured 'pending' error; v0.6.10 will pipe the JSON-RPC envelope to the npx-spawned child's stdin and read its stdout response. Until then, operators wanting tool-call traffic should keep mcpGateway.type='mcpjungle' on hosts where they have an MCPJungle binary configured.
PHASE-12 follow-up: bootstrapper auto-registers URL ACL via netsh http add urlacl during install so the native gateway works in console mode without elevation.
PHASE-13: Win2D / Direct2D rendering in the WinUI shell. Plan file shipped in v0.6.8.

history.summary: The biggest operator-pain item is gone: pool definitions now persist across service restart and MSI upgrade. AppConfiguration carries a pools field (mirror of WorkerSupervisor::pools_), the runtime hydrates it at boot, and pool admin routes write back to disk on every upsert / remove. Operator no longer loses the 7 carefully-configured pools every time MCOS restarts. Plus: per-instance graphic telemetry sparkline charts on the dashboard's Pools deck (Canvas, GPU-composited automatically), telemetry events ring producer wired (supervisor lifecycle + lease router fire structured events into /api/telemetry/events instead of leaving the ring empty), and /api/telemetry/events now returns the {events, maxEvents} envelope the dashboard expects + honors ?max=N. PHASE-12 (full native HTTP.sys gateway, replacing MCPJungle dependency) and PHASE-13 (Win2D / Direct2D rendering in the shell) plan files written.
entries: feat(persistence): AppConfiguration gains a pools field (NLOHMANN_DEFINE_TYPE_NON_INTRUSIVE_WITH_DEFAULT) so pool definitions are mirrored to disk in the persisted config file. Instance state (PIDs, Ready/Busy lifecycle, sampled telemetry) is intentionally NOT persisted -- supervised processes don't survive a restart by design (Job Object KILL_ON_JOB_CLOSE), so re-reading stale instance rows would lie. The runtime re-creates instances on demand from scalePolicy.minInstances after load.

feat(persistence): runtime reads configuration.pools at boot and calls workerSupervisor_->upsertPool(...) for each. Hydrates after configurationService_ is constructed but before the worker supervisor accepts external requests, so the API surface sees the persisted pools immediately on startup.

feat(persistence): POST /api/pools and POST /api/pools/{id}/remove now call persistSupervisedPoolsToConfiguration() after the in-memory operation succeeds. Helper snapshots WorkerSupervisor::pools_, strips instance state, and sends the updated AppConfiguration through configurationService_->update() which triggers the persisted-config-file write.

feat(dashboard): per-instance live sparkline charts on the Pools deck. New paintInstanceSparklines() walks every .instance-spark canvas and draws CPU% (cyan accent, 0-100 fixed range) and Memory MB (cyan-blue, dynamic range) time-series. State.instanceHistory[instanceId] keeps a 60-sample ring per instance (~2 minutes at the 2 s polling cadence). Browser GPU-composites the canvas automatically.
feat(dashboard-css): .instance-spark + .instance-telemetry-table styles for the new charts. 120x32 canvas with subtle Tron-themed border and translucent radial gradient background.

feat(telemetry): /api/pools route handlers (POST /api/pools, /scale, /drain, /remove, lease acquire) emit structured TelemetryEvent records into the telemetryAggregator_ on success. Through v0.6.7 the events ring stayed empty because no producer was wired; v0.6.8 makes Recent Telemetry Events on the dashboard's Overview deck and /api/telemetry/events both populate with real lifecycle activity.

fix(telemetry): /api/telemetry/events now returns the {events, maxEvents} envelope the dashboard JavaScript reads. Through v0.6.7 the route returned a raw array, the dashboard's state.telemetryEvents.events was therefore undefined, and the Overview deck rendered an empty list. Also: ?max=N query parsing was missing entirely; the route only matched the bare path. Fixed both.

feat(shell): the Configuration Narrative panel now surfaces activeProfile.preferredBindAddress so operators can confirm what LAN clients see in /api/discovery and /.well-known/mcos.json. Through v0.6.7 the panel showed bindAddress (the listen target, often 0.0.0.0) but not the advertised IP override.

docs(phases): PHASE-12 plan file (handoff/realignment/PHASE-12-native-http-sys-gateway.md) -- full native HTTP.sys MCP gateway in C++, replacing the v0.6.7 honest-503 listener with a real implementation. ADR-003 trigger fired. Estimated 1-2 weeks for MVP, 4-8 weeks for production-ready. NativeHttpSysGatewayAdapter alongside the existing McpJungleGatewayAdapter; mcpGateway.type switches between them.

docs(phases): PHASE-13 plan file (handoff/realignment/PHASE-13-direct2d-shell-rendering.md) -- Win2D / Direct2D rendering for high-rate live visuals in the WinUI shell. Per-instance telemetry charts at 60Hz, Tron grid backdrop as procedural shader, activity stream as scrolling SwapChainPanel, saturation rings with smooth interpolation. WinUI 3 already runs on DX11 via Composition; PHASE-13 cuts the four high-frequency surfaces out of XAML's layout pass.
docs(version): bump 0.6.7 -> 0.6.8 (pool persistence + per-instance graphic telemetry + telemetry events ring + shell config narrative + PHASE-12 + PHASE-13 plans)
knownIssuesDeferredToNextRelease: PHASE-12 (full native HTTP.sys gateway in C++) -- replaces the v0.6.7 honest-503 listener with a real gateway. Plan file shipped this release; implementation in a future release.

PHASE-13 (Win2D / Direct2D rendering in the WinUI shell) -- per-instance live charts at 60Hz, Tron grid as procedural shader, activity stream as scrolling SwapChainPanel. Plan file shipped this release; implementation in a future release.

history.summary: MCP Gateway port stops returning TCP RST. Option D from the PHASE-11 evaluation memo: when the gateway adapter is in Disabled or supervised-mock mode (no MCPJungle binary configured, the default state), McpJungleGatewayAdapter now binds the configured listen port (default 8080) with a tiny accept loop that returns a structured HTTP 503 'Service Unavailable' JSON to every request. LAN AI clients pointing at the advertised gateway URL get a useful error with operator guidance instead of connection refused. The placeholder listener releases the port the moment a real MCPJungle binary is about to be spawned, and re-binds it after Stop() so the gateway port stays answerable across the entire adapter lifecycle. Replaced wholesale by PHASE-12's native HTTP.sys gateway when that lands.
entries: feat(gateway): McpJungleGatewayAdapter starts an honest 503 listener on mcpGateway.listenHost:listenPort whenever no real binary is supervising the port. Returns Content-Type: application/json with adapterState, guidance, and a pointer at /api/gateway/status for full diagnostic state. Eliminates the connection-refused experience LAN AI clients hit when pointing at the advertised gateway URL before MCPJungle is configured.

feat(gateway): listener integrates cleanly with the lifecycle: started on enter-Disabled, started on enter-supervised-mock-Running, stopped on enter-real-binary-Running (so MCPJungle's bind() doesn't fail with EADDRINUSE), restarted on Stop(), torn down in the destructor. WSAStartup is refcounted so the adapter is safe to construct alongside other Winsock consumers.

feat(gateway): bind failures (e.g., port already in use by a stale MCPJungle from a previous run) silently skip listener creation rather than escalating. The adapter's own state surface in /api/gateway/status is the source of truth; the listener is best-effort sugar.
docs(version): bump 0.6.6 -> 0.6.7 (Option D: gateway port honest-503 listener)
knownIssuesDeferredToNextRelease: Pool definitions live in WorkerSupervisor::pools_ (memory only) and are lost on every service restart / upgrade. AppConfiguration doesn't carry a pools field. Refactor planned for v0.6.8.

Per-instance graphic telemetry charts (CPU/RAM/disk-IO) for sub-agents — Canvas-based mini-charts on each pool card in the browser dashboard, plus formal PHASE-13 plan for Win2D/Direct2D charts in the WinUI shell.

Telemetry events ring at /api/telemetry/events returns count=0 because no producer is wired to it (only the activity ring is populated). Supervisor lifecycle + lease router events should also feed it.
PHASE-12 (full native HTTP.sys MCP gateway in C++) — replaces the honest-503 listener with a real gateway. Plan file forthcoming.

history.summary: Fixes the MsiInstaller Error 1309 dialog every operator saw on upgrade ('Error reading from file: ...Master Control Orchestration Server.lnk, system error 5'). Cause: WiX wrote the Start Menu and Desktop shortcuts as non-advertised (file-content) shortcuts, so during MajorUpgrade the runtime tried to checksum the existing .lnk before overwriting it; Explorer or Search Indexer often had a handle on the .lnk, the read returned ACCESS_DENIED, and Windows surfaced the 1309 dialog. Switched both shortcuts to Advertise=yes (no physical .lnk to lock; Windows resolves them at click-time) and added util:CloseApplication entries so the Restart Manager politely closes the WinUI shell + launcher before file overwrites. Also: dashboard activity ring now drops malformed-request entries (empty method/path from port-scanner probes were polluting the Live Command Stream); host telemetry's primaryIpAddress now honors activeProfile.preferredBindAddress so the WinUI shell + dashboard host-network panel match what /api/discovery and DNS-SD advertise (v0.6.4 fixed this for advertise-side only).
entries: fix(installer): switched StartMenuShortcut + DesktopShortcut to Advertise=yes. Eliminates the MsiInstaller Error 1309 dialog every upgrade — the .lnk no longer exists as a physical file to lock; Windows resolves advertised shortcuts via the registry at click-time. Trade-off is a single MSI repair on first click after install (transparent).

fix(installer): added util:CloseApplication entries for MasterControlShell.exe and MasterControlOrchestrationServer.exe (the launcher). MajorUpgrade now uses Restart Manager to politely send WM_CLOSE to those processes before file overwrites, with a 15s grace period before force-termination. Belt-and-suspenders fix on top of the advertised-shortcut change.

fix(runtime): activity ring drops admin_api_request entries with empty method+path. Through v0.6.5 the SimpleHttpServer parsed bare-TCP-connect probes (port scanners, half-open keep-alive checks) into HttpRequest{method='', path=''} which then landed in the activity ring as '  -> 200 (0ms)' rows polluting the dashboard's Live Command Stream. Guarded the append site so only well-formed requests are recorded.

fix(runtime): host telemetry primaryIpAddress honors activeProfile.preferredBindAddress. v0.6.4 fixed this for the discovery-doc / DNS-SD advertise side; v0.6.6 carries the same override through to the dashboard snapshot's telemetry block so the WinUI shell environment narrative + the dashboard host-network panel match what LAN clients see.
docs(version): bump 0.6.5 -> 0.6.6 (uninstaller fix + activity ring polish + telemetry IP override)
knownIssuesDeferredToNextRelease: Pool definitions live in WorkerSupervisor::pools_ (memory only) and are lost on every service restart / upgrade. AppConfiguration doesn't carry a pools field. v0.6.7 will add it, wire load-on-start, and write-back-on-upsert.

Telemetry events ring at /api/telemetry/events returns count=0 because no producer is wired to it (only the activity ring at /api/activity is populated). v0.6.7 will route supervisor lifecycle + lease router events into the telemetry events ring.

Per-instance graphic telemetry (CPU / RAM / disk-IO charts) for each pool instance is requested but not yet implemented. v0.6.7 will add Canvas-based mini-charts to the pool cards on the dashboard's Pools deck.

MCP Gateway TCP 8080 has no listener until MCPJungle is supervised. Until then, LAN clients pointing at http://<host>:8080/mcp get connection refused. Honest-fallback per ADR-002 §9 + PHASE-02. v0.6.7 will surface a clearer dashboard hint.

history.summary: Real-time per-instance CPU/RAM telemetry for every supervised worker, plus the Apply Host Settings button is finally clickable. The shell's MainWindow.xaml.cpp had a stale gate at the attach-runtime call site that excluded kSettingsView and kOverviewView; SettingsSectionControl::AttachRuntime therefore never fired and the Apply button stayed greyed out forever. Same bug also explained why the Claude Code Control toggle on the shell's Overview was unresponsive. WorkerSupervisor now samples GetProcessTimes (kernel + user FILETIMEs) and GetProcessMemoryInfo (working set) for every supervised child PID, computes delta-CPU% over the polling interval normalized by host core count, and surfaces real numbers through EndpointInstance.telemetry on every /api/pools read. Dashboard cadence dropped to 2s to match the WinUI shell's live tick.
entries: fix(shell): MainWindow attachInteractiveRuntime call site now includes kSettingsView and kOverviewView. v0.6.3 shipped with both missing -- Settings Apply Host Settings button stayed disabled because runtime_ stayed nullptr, and the Claude Code Control toggle on the Overview never got the runtime attached either.

feat(supervisor): WorkerSupervisor samples per-instance CPU% and working-set MB for every supervised child PID. ChildProcess gains a FILETIME baseline (lastKernelTime/lastUserTime/lastSampleTime) that sampleProcessLoadLocked uses to compute delta-CPU normalized by host logical-core count; first sample establishes baseline (0.0%), subsequent samples produce real readings clamped to [0, 100]. GetProcessMemoryInfo's WorkingSetSize is reported in MB (matches Task Manager's Memory column).

feat(supervisor): findPool() and listPools() now refresh telemetry for every instance before returning. Sampling happens under the supervisor's mutex_ on each call; the dashboard's 2s polling cadence yields a fresh reading every other tick.
feat(dashboard): refresh interval dropped from 5000ms to 2000ms to match the WinUI shell's live tick and keep the new per-instance telemetry visibly real-time.
build(runtime): MasterControlApp links psapi for GetProcessMemoryInfo and #include <psapi.h> in the Win32 includes block.
docs(version): bump 0.6.4 -> 0.6.5 (real-time per-instance telemetry + shell Apply fix)

history.summary: Operator-set IP wins. Both the discovery document (/.well-known/mcos.json + /api/discovery) and the Win32 DNS-SD registration now consult activeProfile.preferredBindAddress before falling back to auto-detected primaryIpAddress. Without this, on dual-stack Windows hosts the runtime's GetAdaptersAddresses(AF_UNSPEC) enumeration often surfaced the IPv6 ULA first, and LAN clients saw an unreachable IPv6 address (or one their stack didn't route to) instead of the IPv4 LAN address the operator pinned in mcos.json.
entries: fix(discovery): DiscoveryService::currentDocument honors activeProfile.preferredBindAddress as the highest-precedence source for serverIpAddress + every URL it builds (gateway, onboarding, governance). Auto-detected primaryIpAddress is now a fallback, not the default.

fix(dns-sd): the DnsServiceRegister site that publishes _mcos._tcp.local + _mcos-mcp._tcp.local + _mcos-onboarding._tcp.local applies the same precedence chain so DNS-SD advertises the operator-chosen IP.
docs(version): bump 0.6.3 -> 0.6.4 (advertise-IP correctness)

history.summary: Claude Code Control becomes a real toggle switch on the Overview deck of both GUI surfaces, and the runtime resolver gains two more fallback paths that fix the silent-toggle case where the service runs as SYSTEM but WTSQueryUserToken returns ERROR_NO_TOKEN (locked console, RDP-only login, fresh boot). The WinUI desktop shell's card moves out of Settings and onto the Overview page, replacing the previous Connect / Disconnect button with a ToggleSwitch (OnContent='Connected', OffContent='Disconnected'). The browser dashboard's button is replaced with a CSS toggle switch backed by an accessible <input type='checkbox'>. Both surfaces refresh from /api/claude-plugin/status on load and on every snapshot tick, and the toggle stays interactive even when the runtime would refuse so failures are visible rather than silent.
entries: feat(shell-overview): Claude Code Control card with a ToggleSwitch lives at the top of OverviewSectionControl. Toggled handler dispatches ToggleClaudePluginAsync; suspendClaudePluginToggleHandler_ short-circuits programmatic IsOn writes from snapshot refresh so they don't re-fire the handler.
feat(shell-overview): OverviewSectionControl gains AttachRuntime; MainWindow attachInteractiveRuntime now wires the runtime to the Overview view in addition to the existing interactive sections.
remove(shell-settings): Claude Code Control card removed from SettingsSectionControl; the corresponding async/render helpers and member fields are deleted.

feat(dashboard): Claude Code Control card uses an HTML toggle switch (<input type='checkbox'> styled as a track + thumb via CSS sibling selectors). The toggle binds 'change' instead of 'click' so keyboard activation works. The toggle stays enabled even when activeUserResolved is false, so the user always sees feedback; refusal is shown in the status text.

feat(dashboard-css): new .toggle-switch / .toggle-track / .toggle-label styles. Track is 52x26 px with a 20 px thumb; checked state uses the cyan accent (#1cf2c1) plus a 12 px glow. Native checkbox visually hidden but keyboard-focusable.

fix(runtime): four-path active-user resolver. Path 1 = process USERPROFILE env var (covers --console / shell-hosted). Path 2 = WTSGetActiveConsoleSessionId + WTSQueryUserToken (canonical SYSTEM-service path). Path 3 = WTSEnumerateSessionsExW iterating every session looking for State==Active with a user name (covers RDP-only logons, locked console with active RDP). Path 4 = enumerate C:\Users\* directories with a .claude subfolder and pick the most-recently-modified one (last-resort coverage when no live token is available). Without paths 3-4, the toggle silently returned errno 1008 ERROR_NO_TOKEN even though a usable user profile was on disk.
docs(version): bump 0.6.2 -> 0.6.3 (UI uplift + resolver fallbacks)

history.summary: Claude Code Control toggle now also lives in the WinUI desktop shell's Settings panel, and the runtime's active-user resolver works in console mode. v0.6.1 only added the toggle to the browser dashboard, and the resolver assumed the runtime had SYSTEM privilege (true for the Windows service path, false for --console / shell-launched runs). When MCOS was hosted in console mode the toggle returned errno 1008 (ERROR_NO_TOKEN) because WTSQueryUserToken needs SE_TCB_NAME and non-service processes don't carry it. v0.6.2 adds a dual-path resolver that prefers GetEnvironmentVariableW("USERPROFILE") and only falls through to WTSQueryUserToken when the env var resolves to the SYSTEM profile.
entries: feat(shell): SettingsSectionControl gains a Claude Code Control card at the top of the Settings page mirroring the dashboard card; refreshes on AttachRuntime and on every snapshot tick; async toggle handler with busy state

feat(shell-runtime): ShellRuntime::FetchClaudePluginStatus + ToggleClaudePlugin call /api/claude-plugin/{status,toggle} through the existing httpRequest pipeline; ShellClaudePluginStatus carries reachable / registered / activeUserResolved / userName / profileDir / source / target / lastError / transportError

fix(runtime): dual-path active-user resolver. Path 1 reads GetEnvironmentVariableW("USERPROFILE") and uses it when it is not the SYSTEM profile (covers --console mode, shell-launched runs, any non-service host). Path 2 retains WTSGetActiveConsoleSessionId + WTSQueryUserToken + CreateEnvironmentBlock for the SYSTEM-hosted service path. Without this fix the toggle failed in console mode with errno 1008 ERROR_NO_TOKEN because WTSQueryUserToken requires SE_TCB_NAME which only SYSTEM holds
docs(version): bump 0.6.1 -> 0.6.2 (operator-feature minor + console-mode fix)

history.summary: One-click Claude Code control. The MSI now bundles the operator-side mcos-control Claude Code plugin, and the dashboard's Overview deck gains a Claude Code Control card with a Connect / Disconnect toggle. Toggling Connect drops a directory junction at the active console user's %USERPROFILE%\.claude\plugins\mcos-control pointing at the install directory's bundled plugin source, so Claude Code picks up the bridge MCP server, sub-agents, slash commands, and skill on next launch. The runtime resolves the active interactive user via WTSGetActiveConsoleSessionId + WTSQueryUserToken + CreateEnvironmentBlock, so the toggle works whether MCOS is running as the Windows service (SYSTEM) or in --console mode. Disconnecting removes only the junction, never the install source.
entries: feat(installer): MSI bundles the mcos-control Claude Code plugin under share/claude-plugins/mcos-control plus a Register-McosControlPlugin.ps1 helper at the install root for manual / scripted registration
feat(runtime): GET /api/claude-plugin/status returns {registered, activeUserResolved, userName, profileDir, source, target, lastError} for the active console user

feat(runtime): POST /api/claude-plugin/toggle flips registration via cmd /c mklink /J for create and RemoveDirectoryW for remove; uses WTSGetActiveConsoleSessionId + WTSQueryUserToken + CreateEnvironmentBlock to recover the interactive user's USERPROFILE even when the runtime is hosted as a Windows service running under SYSTEM

feat(dashboard): Overview deck adds a Claude Code Control card with Connect / Disconnect toggle, live status text (connected / disconnected / no active user), and active-user attribution; busy state suppresses double-clicks

docs(wiki): Claude-Code-Plugin documents the dashboard toggle as the primary install path; Packaging-and-Gateway-Binary lists the plugin payload and helper script as MSI-installed; Maintenance covers junction-vs-copy upgrade behavior
docs(version): bump 0.6.0 -> 0.6.1 (operator-feature minor)

history.summary: Gateway-first MCP realignment (ADR-002, PHASE-00 through PHASE-10). MCOS is no longer a direct AI executor; it is a Windows-native LAN MCP Gateway host. AI clients connect to one advertised MCP endpoint and consume server-generated onboarding profiles, governance bundles, and supervised worker pools. Strategy=minor-on-architecture-change applies because the public AI-client surface fundamentally changed but the operator surface from ADR-001 (LAN Client Control Plane) is preserved as a coexisting operator path.
